what_it_does: This file is an append-only, race-safe message channel used by autonomous agents (guided-review, cognition, the-runner) to coordinate work, announce state, request unblocks, flag blockers, record commits and convergence proofs, and maintain shared protocol.
observations: The top of the file gives shell instructions for safe concurrent appending (`cat >> MESSAGES.md`) and forbids read-then-edit.
Messages are tagged with date, sender, recipient, and a `re:` subject line, newest at the bottom.
The chronology spans 2026-06-08 through 2026-06-10 and mixes protocol negotiation, build progress, blockers, corrections, and proofs.
Three agents appear: guided-review (surface/FE/wire), cognition (engine/MCP/roles), and coherence (gates/drift/orphans).
Many messages cite exact git commit hashes, suite counts (e.g., 119/0, 120/0), and file-disjoint lane ownership.
A major thread is registry generation (RG1–RG10), culminating in a full-run proof with 940 candidates, reconciliation, and a 4-stop approval walk.
Later threads cover GC1–GC15 capabilities, operator_memory of Tim, flows registry, drift-radar, floor-walk, and eval cycles (P-series, Q-series).
Honest corrections are first-class content (e.g., 'CORRECTION to my last post', 'CORRECTION²').
Tim is repeatedly referenced as the human operator/approver with pending gates (e.g., 18-row block-confirm, Track-1 brain conversation).
The file records discovered protocol hazards: concurrent-commit race, staged-index contamination, dead builders leaving uncommitted edits.
standouts: It is a real-time, human-readable coordination substrate for autonomous builders inside the repo itself.
RG10 registry-generation convergence is proven by use: 23 mockups, 940 candidates, deterministic floor catching invented IDs, and a refined 4-decision approval walk.
The no-confidence law is enforced: `confidence` fields removed from role schemas, replaced with grounding tags, plus a standing gate test.
An escalation shows the protocol under stress: guided-review blocked when cognition held `suite.py` and did not flip a one-line assertion.
The file ends with the tree declared clean and the loop idling after a 'wide pass' adopted stray work.
conventions: Append-only, bottom-newest; no editing of prior messages.
Explicit commit hashes and gate counts are used as evidence.
Blockers are classified by flag-tier: structural (can block) vs semantic (propose, owner adjudicates).
File-disjoint lane ownership is asserted repeatedly (e.g., cognition.py/roles.py vs surface/FE vs gate files).
Explicit-pathspec commits (`git commit -- <paths>`) are promoted to avoid concurrent-index races.
Corrections are appended rather than editing prior messages.
concerns: The file is very long and jargon-dense, making it hard for a newcomer to parse without external context.
Multiple appended corrections indicate earlier messages were stale or wrong, which could mislead readers who stop mid-file.
Some messages are emotionally loaded/escalatory, which may obscure factual content.
Many references to external files (AUTONOMOUS-LOOP.md, WORK-SPLIT.md, CONVERGENCE-ROUND.md, etc.) are not shown here.
The boundary between coordination log and build journal is blurry; the file serves both purposes.
notes: An append-only, agent-authored coordination log that doubles as a build journal, ending with a clean tree and idle loop.
questions: What are the precise responsibilities and code boundaries of cognition, guided-review, and coherence?
What do the many acronyms (RG, GC, G, B, A, C, M, P, Q) stand for and how are they scoped?
What is the actual content of the referenced protocol files (AUTONOMOUS-LOOP.md, WORK-SPLIT.md, CONVERGENCE-ROUND.md)?
What is the final state of Tim's pending gates (18-row operator-memory block-confirm, Track-1 brain conversation, [M] walk surface)?
Which of the reported commits and suite counts remain accurate at the current HEAD?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose as an append-only three-session coordination channel, but it also functions as a build journal, escalation record, and convergence proof ledger.
apparent_themes: Autonomous multi-agent coordination without human relay
Convergence by use and deterministic floor checks
Explicit, atomic commits to avoid shared-tree races
Operator approval gates and continuous self-improvement
intention_signals: The file is trying to let three autonomous builders stay aligned, avoid collisions, surface blockers quickly, record decisions and proofs, and converge on a shared main branch through protocol discipline and by-use verification.
novelty: A repo file used as a human-readable message bus for autonomous agents, with shell-append anti-race instructions and candid self-reported failures/dead builders as first-class content.
contribution: A durable, timestamped, append-only record of cross-agent coordination, blockers, decisions, and convergence proofs; the tagging, explicit-pathspec, file-disjoint-lane, and honest-correction conventions; and the recorded outcomes of registry generation, operator memory, flows, drift-radar, and floor-walk.
summary_for_embedding: An append-only, race-safe message channel in `company/build-prep/coordination/MESSAGES.md` where autonomous agents coordinate work, report blockers, record commits, and prove convergence over several days.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the shared coordination substrate for autonomous builders, replacing human relay with an auditable, append-only log of state, requests, and proofs.
